perturbation.original_text: 9. Termination: The covenants contained in this agreement shall terminate and expire on the date that is the first anniversary of the date first set out above on this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Termination: The covenants contained in this agreement shall terminate and expire on the date that is the first anniversary of the date first set out above on this agreement, unless extended by mutual written consent of both parties. Either party may terminate this agreement with a 30-day written notice, regardless of the anniversary date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a conflict regarding the termination of the agreement. The original text states it terminates on the first anniversary. The modified text adds that it can be terminated with 30-day notice by either party, regardless of the anniversary date, creating an in-text contradiction. This creates uncertainty about when and how the agreement can be terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination: The covenants contained in this agreement shall terminate and expire on the date that is the first anniversary of the date first set out above on this agreement, unless extended by mutual written consent of both parties. Either party may terminate this agreement with a 30-day written notice, regardless of the anniversary date.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a fixed termination date (first anniversary). The changed text introduces an additional termination condition (30-day notice), which contradicts the original fixed termination date. The contradicted text is an exact copy of the changed text which causes an in-text contradiction because one part of the contract is contradicting another part of the contract, or in this case the same part contradicts itself. This satisfies the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections (or in this case, the same section due to the nature of the modification) provide conflicting conditions regarding termination, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information: During the period commencing on the date of this agreement and ending on the date of termination in section 9, the Recipient will take all commercially reasonable measures to ensure the continued confidentiality of the Confidential Information and shall not disclose it to anyone except (i) to the Recipient’s Representatives under the limited terms and conditions set forth in section 3, or (ii) as permitted under section 4.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information: During the period commencing on the date of this agreement and continuing indefinitely, the Recipient will take all commercially reasonable measures to ensure the continued confidentiality of the Confidential Information and shall not disclose it to anyone except (i) to the Recipient’s Representatives under the limited terms and conditions set forth in section 3, or (ii) as permitted under section 4.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a conflict regarding the duration of the non-disclosure obligation. Originally, it was tied to the termination date in section 9. By changing 'ending on the date of termination in section 9' to 'continuing indefinitely,' the non-disclosure obligation is now perpetual. This contradicts section 9, creating uncertainty about whether the confidentiality obligations cease upon termination of the agreement or continue indefinitely.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination: The covenants contained in this agreement shall terminate and expire on the date that is the first anniversary of the date first set out above on this agreement, unless extended by mutual written consent of both parties. Either party may terminate this agreement with a 30-day written notice, regardless of the anniversary date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of the non-disclosure agreement from a period ending on the date of termination in section 9 to continuing indefinitely. The contradicted text in the Termination section specifies that covenants in the agreement terminate on the first anniversary, unless extended. This creates a direct conflict because the non-disclosure obligation, as modified, could extend beyond the termination date specified in the contradicted text, which introduces a strong in-text contradiction, thus adhering to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 1. Confidential Information: “Confidential Information” means all information which is non-public, confidential or proprietary in nature, whether transferred in writing, orally, visually, electronically or by other means, disclosed by the Corporation or its representatives to the Recipient on or after the date hereof and for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Confidential Information: “Confidential Information” means all information which is non-public, confidential or proprietary in nature, whether transferred in writing, orally, visually, electronically or by other means, disclosed by the Corporation or its representatives to the Recipient on or after the date hereof and for the Purpose. However, any information related to financial projections shall not be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The change adds an exception to the definition of 'Confidential Information,' stating that financial projections are not considered confidential. This contradicts other sections (e.g., Section 2) that assume all information disclosed for the Purpose is confidential, creating an in-text contradiction regarding which information is subject to non-disclosure obligations. This could lead to disputes about the use of financial projections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-disclosure of Confidential Information: During the period commencing on the date of this agreement and continuing indefinitely, the Recipient will take all commercially reasonable measures to ensure the continued confidentiality of the Confidential Information and shall not disclose it to anyone except (i) to the Recipient’s Representatives under the limited terms and conditions set forth in section 3, or (ii) as permitted under section 4.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the definition of 'Confidential Information,' explicitly excluding financial projections. The contradicted text states that 'the Recipient will take all commercially reasonable measures to ensure the continued confidentiality of the Confidential Information'. Because the definition of Confidential information now excludes financial projections, the recipient is no longer obligated to keep financial projection confidential, directly contradicting the non-disclosure obligation in the contradicted text. This is a strong, in-text contradiction because it creates a conflicting understanding of what information is subject to non-disclosure obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Return of Confidential Information: As soon as practicable after receipt of a written notice from the Corporation to the Recipient, the Recipient shall (i) at its election, either destroy or return to the Corporation all Confidential Information furnished by the Corporation which is in tangible or electronic form, including any copies which the Recipient or its Representatives have made, and (ii) certify to the Corporation, in writing, that the Recipient has done the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Return of Confidential Information: As soon as practicable after receipt of a written notice from the Corporation to the Recipient, the Recipient may, at its discretion, retain all Confidential Information furnished by the Corporation which is in tangible or electronic form, including any copies which the Recipient or its Representatives have made, and the Recipient is not obligated to certify to the Corporation, in writing, that the Recipient has destroyed or returned the information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return or destruction of confidential information upon written notice. The modified text gives the Recipient the discretion to retain the information and removes the obligation to certify compliance. This contradicts Section 2, which requires the Recipient to ensure the continued confidentiality of the Information, and Section 3, which defines the purpose of the Confidential Information. It is now uncertain whether the recipient has to return the information or maintain confidentiality after the agreement is terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-disclosure of Confidential Information: During the period commencing on the date of this agreement and continuing indefinitely, the Recipient will take all commercially reasonable measures to ensure the continued confidentiality of the Confidential Information and shall not disclose it to anyone except (i) to the Recipient’s Representatives under the limited terms and conditions set forth in section 3, or (ii) as permitted under section 4.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 gives the Recipient the discretion to retain confidential information and removes the obligation to certify compliance. The contradicted text ('Non-disclosure of Confidential Information') states that the Recipient will take all commercially reasonable measures to ensure the continued confidentiality of the Confidential Information indefinitely. The change creates a direct conflict because the Recipient is no longer required to return or destroy confidential information, making it uncertain whether they have to maintain its confidentiality. This violates the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations, leading to in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Remedies: The Recipient understands and agrees that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives and that, in addition to all other remedies, the Corporation shall be entitled to specific performance or injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Remedies: The Recipient understands and agrees that money damages shall be the sole and exclusive remedy for any breach of this agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives and that, in addition to all other remedies, the Corporation shall not be entitled to specific performance or injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for specific performance or injunctive relief. The modified text states that money damages are the sole remedy and specifically prohibits specific performance or injunctive relief. This creates a direct conflict with Section 10 by limiting the type of remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies: The Recipient understands and agrees that money damages shall be the sole and exclusive remedy for any breach of this agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives and that, in addition to all other remedies, the Corporation shall not be entitled to specific performance or injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that money damages are the sole and exclusive remedy, while the contradicted text repeats the same statement. The original text allowed for other remedies like specific performance or injunctive relief, which directly contradicts the changed and contradicted texts. Therefore, the changed text causes a direct contradiction within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Assignment: Neither party may assign any of its rights or obligations under this agreement without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Assignment: The Corporation may assign any of its rights or obligations under this agreement to any affiliate without the prior written consent of the other party. The Recipient may not assign any of its rights or obligations under this agreement without the prior written consent of the Corporation.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states neither party can assign rights without consent. The modified text states the Corporation can assign to affiliates without consent, while the Recipient still needs consent. This creates a conflict within Section 15 itself regarding assignment rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment: The Corporation may assign any of its rights or obligations under this agreement to any affiliate without the prior written consent of the other party. The Recipient may not assign any of its rights or obligations under this agreement without the prior written consent of the Corporation.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits assignment by either party without consent. The changed text allows the Corporation to assign rights to affiliates without consent, while the Recipient still needs consent. The 'Contradicted Text' is identical to the 'Changed Text' which demonstrates that the change introduces an inconsistency within Section 15 itself by creating different assignment rules for the Corporation and the Recipient. This directly contradicts the original uniformly restrictive clause and is a strong in-text contradiction as it creates conflicting obligations based on which party seeks to assign their rights.
